name: Labguru Assistant (Cenevo)
product: AI assistant embedded in the Labguru ELN/LIMS platform
category: AI copilot embedded in existing ELN/LIMS

baseline.summary: Labguru Assistant remains embedded directly into the Labguru ELN/LIMS platform, but the parent company has undergone a major corporate change: BioData's Labguru business combined with Titian Software (both properties of Digital Science, backed by Battery Ventures) to form a new unified entity now branded 'Cenevo,' with Keith Hale appointed Group CEO in January 2025. Under this new branding, Cenevo relaunched/reintroduced the Labguru Assistant in September 2025 as an AI-enabled extension for real-time, in-platform scientific support.
keyFeatures: Contextual suggestions on concentrations, incubation times, and controls, generating structured protocols ready to save within Labguru
Real-time support for protocol deviations, equipment issues, and unexpected data
Explainable, traceable, fully auditable insights aimed at reproducibility and regulatory-grade compliance
Can search a lab's entire workspace (ELN entries, inventory, protocols) and connect to trusted external databases for deeper scientific insights
Marketed as 'purpose-built for the complexity of life science' rather than a generic chatbot, always-on and always-in-context
baseline.recentNews: The most significant recent development is corporate consolidation: Labguru/BioData merged with Titian Software under Battery Ventures ownership to form 'Cenevo,' a combined tier-1 lab-management/sample-management platform (Keith Hale named Group CEO, Jan 2025); Cenevo then issued a September 2025 press release relaunching Labguru Assistant, emphasizing it is 'not a separate system or tool' but always-on, in-context, and accountable AI embedded across the platform, now reportedly serving 950+ customers (including 8 of the top 10 pharma companies) and 45,000+ scientist users worldwide.
sources: Cenevo Debuts AI Assistant for Life Science Research
Cenevo (previously Titian Software UK and Labguru) - Crunchbase

name: R-COP (ThinkBio.Ai)
product: R-COP, an AI research copilot for biomedical R&D
category: Standalone AI copilot spanning discovery-to-trial workflows

baseline.summary: R-COP is ThinkBio.Ai's AI-powered platform unifying experimental design, protocol generation, clinical study planning, and lab operations analytics into one system, aimed at eliminating manual bottlenecks across biomedical R&D.
keyFeatures: Generates execution-ready experimental protocols and refines them based on context
Optimizes clinical study frameworks with regulatory-readiness features
Harmonizes lab data across LIMS, ERP, CRM, and lab instruments
Positions itself across discovery, development, AND clinical planning (broader scope than a pure lab-protocol tool)
baseline.recentNews: Marketing emphasis in 2026 on being a single unifying layer across fragmented biomedical R&D tools rather than a point solution.

name: Benchling
category: Established R&D cloud platform (broader, less AI-agent-native)

baseline.summary: Benchling is a mature R&D cloud platform used by 1,200+ pharma and biotech companies, covering notebook, registry, and workflow management. Less purpose-built around an AI agent that learns from failed experiments specifically, but the dominant incumbent platform Elnora would be compared against or need to integrate alongside.
keyFeatures: Broad R&D data platform: notebook, molecular biology registry, workflow tools
Large existing customer base across pharma/biotech
AI features exist but are not centered on learning from negative/failed experimental data the way Elnora's core thesis is
baseline.recentNews: Continues to be the default R&D data infrastructure choice for many biotech/pharma companies; competitive pressure is less about AI-agent sophistication and more about being the incumbent system of record.
